channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Sony No Longer Releasing Single-Player PlayStation Exclusives On PC
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve been enjoying playing the likes of God of War, the ‘Ghost’ franchise, and other Sony single-player games on PC, you might need to upgrade to a PS5 for future releases, if you haven’t already.

In a shocking turn of events, Sony is ending the bulk of its third-party exclusivity strategy by taking away future core single-player titles from PC. This means that upcoming games like Wolverine will remain PS5 exclusives, and it surely shuts the door on PlayStation-to-Xbox ports.

Single-Player Sony Titles To Remain PlayStation Exclusives Moving Forward

In a monumental report from Jason Schreier of Bloomberg (paywalled), the journalist has revealed that “Sony Group Corp. no longer plans to release its big PlayStation 5 games on PC.”

Over the last six-plus years, PC players have gratefully accepted ports of Returnal, God of War, God of War Ragnarok, the Spider-Man games, and the Horizon franchise.

Schreier claims that “in recent weeks, PlayStation scrapped plans to bring Ghost of Yotei and other internally developed games to PC.”

On the other hand, multiplayer titles WILL remain as third-party releases, such as Marathon and Marvel Tokon.

A PlayStation spokesperson has apparently refused to comment on this strategic shift. If this comes to fruition, Saros, Wolverine, and future PlayStation franchises, including God of War, Ratchet & Clank, and more, will be sticking to the blue brand for the foreseeable future—although Death Stranding 2 and Kena: Scars of Kosmora are reportedly safe.

Speculation will run rampant over why Sony has pulled off such a huge U-turn. Schreier has postulated that PlayStation exclusive ports not selling well on PC could be to blame, whereas people allegedly connected to Sony think that “Releasing their games on PC risks damaging the console’s brand and will hurt sales of the PlayStation 5 and its successors.”

What do you make of this huge report? Are you a PC owner without a PS5? Will this make you take the plunge for massive Sony exclusives? Please share all your thoughts and more via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Read about Resident Evil Requiem becoming the fastest-selling game in franchise history and Activision going after Call of Duty leakers for more IG news.

MOBILE APP

item.title: CSGO Returns to Steam and Pulls In Thousands of Players
item.content:encoded:
Counter-Strike: Global Offensive, the game that defined an esports generation, has resurfaced on Steam for the first time since September 2023, when Counter-Strike 2 arrived on the scene. CSGO has made a bold return with its own Steam Store page, but it’s not as easy as just searching for it and uncovering it through the platform’s repository.

While you could navigate to CSGO via Counter-Strike 2, this marks the first time in almost three years that the game has boasted a native store page on Valve’s all-digital platform.

Oh, and there’s another caveat. Just because the game is back on the store, that doesn’t mean the official servers are back up.7

How to Find CSGO on Steam

Since September 2023 and the launch of Counter-Strike 2, you’ve only been able to explore CSGO, the legendary game that topped the charts for a decade, via the legacy branch housed in CS2.

At some point in the last day or two, Valve added Counter-Strike: Global Offensive back onto the store, but per a message on the listing, kept it absent from search results. That means that, while you won’t see CSGO in search results, you can still navigate to the page and install the game just fine.

So, how can you find CSGO on Steam? The easiest way is to click this link and head straight to the game’s store page.

The game, which at the time of writing boasted Overwhelmingly Positive reviews, can now be tracked via data aggregation platforms.

At the time of publishing, Counter-Strike: Global Offensive boasted more than 47,500 players. It’s a far cry from the game’s all-time high that numbers closer to the two million mark, but it’s indicative of a resurgence driven by the new CSGO store listing.

For a decade, Counter-Strike: Global Offensive was the number one esports title, supporting a worldwide competitive phenomenon worth tens of millions of dollars. It also facilitated the creation of a ‘skins market’ that was, at one point, worth $5 billion. It moved over in September 2023 to make room for Counter-Strike 2, which has been the top-tier title since.

Are you going to jump back into CSGO on Steam? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out all the upcoming esports events this year

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Fatal Frame 2: Crimson Butterfly Remake Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
If Resident Evil 9 wasn’t enough horror for you, then why not revisit a classic reborn in Fatal Frame 2: Crimson Butterfly Remake? The game has been remade from the ground up, and it should be even scarier than it was back in 2002.

As if that wasn’t enticing enough, numerous gameplay features have been amended or added, too; from enhanced Camera Obscura battles to entire new side stories and areas to explore, it’s the most complete version of Fatal Frame 2—and you won’t have to wait long to play it.

If you’re interested and want to know when you can revisit the haunted village of Minakami, then you’re in the right place, as Insider Gaming has all of the release details for you down below.

When Is Fatal Frame 2 Remake Coming Out? Release Date, Explained

Koei Tecmo Games has announced that the Fatal Frame 2: Crimson Butterfly Remake will release across all available platforms on March 12, 2026.

March is stacked full of exciting titles to keep an eye out for. Marathon launches early in the month, giving fans another extraction shooter to sink their teeth into, whilst Crimson Desert releases just a few weeks later, and some think it’s already going to be a Game Of The Year contender.

  • More Insider Gaming: Starfield PS5 Release Date

Now, it looks like the Fatal Frame 2 Remake is yet another game to consider, especially if you’re a fan of horror. The best part is, even if you played it all the way back on the PS2, so much has been added with the Remake, that it’s almost a necessity to give it another playthrough.

Fatal Frame 2 Remake Release Times

The Fatal Frame 2: Crimson Butterfly Remake is set to launch at 4 PM PT on March 11, 2026.

  • More Insider Gaming: Crimson Desert Release Date Countdown

It seems like if you’re a fan of Fatal Frame 2, the best place to be is on the West Coast of North America, as you’ll get the game earlier than the rest of the world. However, fans from across the globe are bound to be tuning in for its launch, so here’s a table with adjusted times for various time zones.

Are you excited for the Fatal Frame 2 Remake? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming Content, check out the Black Ops Royale release times, as well as the Monster Hunter Stories 3 release times.

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem Becomes Fastest-Selling Game In Franchise History
item.content:encoded:
The good people still love Resident Evil as much as ever in 2026. It turns out that Capcom’s arduous marketing campaign for the game has been a success, as Resident Evil Requiem has already sold over 5 million copies in a ridiculously short space of time.

Resident Evil Requiem has topped Metacritic’s greatest games ever list in terms of user score—and it’s hard to explain how unbelievable this is when you consider this includes ALL games.

The game has gone one better and become the series’ fastest-selling title, too, beating out all the other legendary games in the franchise.

Resident Evil Requiem Sells Over 5 Million Copies In 5 Days

An official Capcom press release from March 4 shows that Resident Evil Requiem “Surpasses 5 Million Units!”

Capcom also added in the press release that the franchise (as of December 31, 2025) has sold over 183 million units, meaning Requiem takes its total closer to 190 million, and it’s likely to surpass 200 million by the end of 2026.

Additionally, renowned leaker Dusk Golem claims that this “makes it the top-selling first week of a RE game ever, overtaking the previous record holder.”

The previous record holder was Resident Evil 6, which clocked in at 4.5 million copies sold in one week, whereas Requiem has cleared 5 million in only five days!

With rumors about Resident Evil Requiem DLC and expansions abound, this might only be the beginning for the terrifying latest installment.

A round of applause is necessary for Resident Evil Requiem.

Have you had a chance to play it yet? Is it worthy of all the praise it’s had so far? Share your thoughts and opinions through the free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Resi 9 news and content, check out how to solve The Final Puzzle in Resident Evil Requiem, how to crack the Roulette Easter Egg, and if the fan-favorite Hunk appears in Requiem.
